title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Detective Superintendent Huishe informs Dr. Arthur Calgary that Mickey has been taken into custody and will be charged within 24 hours. Huishe explains that Mickey has an alibi of being in the garden, but suggests he could have used a large magnolia tree to climb into Darnton's room to commit the murder. Huishe notes that Tina, who is recovering, provided incoherent statements: she named "Mickey," but also said, "The cup is empty" and "The pigeon is on the mast." Calgary remains unconvinced of Mickey's guilt and believes the crime is a "crime of passion."

Calgary returns to Sunset Point at dusk to resolve the situation he initiated. Upon arrival, he encounters Hester, Cressida Lindstrom, and a hostile Kirsten Lindstrom, who blames Calgary for the deaths of Philip Darwent and Little Tina. Calgary insists on speaking to everyone, eventually gathering Leo Agha, Gwendraith Vain, and Mary Darwent in the study. 

Calgary addresses the group, stating his motive is to stop the "disaster of the innocent" caused by his initial arrival. He observes that when he first arrived with news to clear Jack's name, the residents seemed satisfied with Jack's guilt because it provided a convenient explanation for the murder of Mrs. Aigle. He points out that while others justified Jack's actions through his mental state or misfortune, Kirsten Lindstrom was the only one who openly condemned him as "immoral."
